blocks: The first thing to understand is that there is no single "Figma API." There are five overlapping programmatic surfaces,

S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — AUTHENTICATION AND WHAT IT ACT

data.title: AUTHENTICATION AND WHAT IT ACTUALLY CONTROLS
blocks: Every REST call requires an X-Figma-Token header. The value can be a Personal Access Token or an OAuth 2.0 token.
Authentication and What It Actually Cont — Personal Access Tokens are the simpler path. Generated in Figma account settings under Security, they carry exactly the

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — IN YOUR .ENV FILE — NEVER COMM

data.title: IN YOUR .ENV FILE — NEVER COMMITTED
blocks: export FIGMA_TOKEN="figd_..."
In your .env file — never committed — Every CLI tool in this book reads FIGMA_TOKEN from the environment. That name is the stable convention. You will set it

data.title: THE RATE LIMIT ARCHITECTURE.
blocks: Rate limits are the most common reason a working script stops working at scale.
The Rate Limit Architecture — The documentation states that limits are applied per token, vary by endpoint, and vary by plan tier. A 429 Too Many Requ

data.title: THE ENTERPRISE GATE.
blocks: · REST API read access to local variable collections
· REST API write access to variables
· Certain webhook event types may be plan-gated
· Full file graph read access
· Component and style metadata

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — OPTIONAL, USED BY WEBHOOK HAND

data.title: OPTIONAL, USED BY WEBHOOK HANDLERS.
blocks: FIGMA_WEBHOOK_PASSCODE=your-passcode-here
Optional, used by webhook handlers — The file key comes from the Figma file URL. For a URL like the key is abc123XYZdef456.

data.title: .GITIGNORE
blocks: .env .env.local .env.*.local

S09.meta: // SLIDE 09 — WHAT THE PING CANNOT TELL YOU

data.title: WHAT THE PING CANNOT TELL YOU.
blocks: The ping is a health check at a moment in time. It has failure modes worth knowing.
What the Ping Cannot Tell You — A shallow file fetch with ?depth=1 succeeds even on files that are too large to fully fetch without timing out. The ping
